title: kb verify <page> — bump verified_against in one command
description:
FIRST: 'git merge main --no-edit'; verify T1's surfaced cursor exists.

Add 'kb.py verify <page> [--note <text>]': set the page's frontmatter verified_against to the current HEAD short-sha and append a Timeline entry ('- <date> — re-verified @<sha>' + optional note). Sandbox <page> to the wiki (resolve + relative_to). Also clear the page from the surfaced cursor (T1) so it can re-surface if it drifts again. This turns clearing a re-read STALE page into one command (STALE→FRESH).

Test (temp wiki):
- a stale page (covers changed since verified_against) → 'kb.py verify <page>' → frontmatter verified_against == HEAD; a Timeline 're-verified' line appended; 'kb status' shows it fresh; surfaced cursor entry cleared.
- traversal on <page> → error.
- py_compile passes.
acceptance_criteria: kb verify <page> bumps verified_against to HEAD + appends a Timeline entry + clears the surfaced-cursor entry; page becomes fresh; path sandboxed; py_compile passes
